chapter_question: How does the metric curvature formula arise from circulation?
inspection_targets: Curvature appears as circulation around shrinking coordinate cells.
Scale factors define the coordinate field being circulated.
The limit of boundary circulation yields local curvature.
The flat case sets the zero-curvature baseline.

source_span.focus: Proves the metric curvature formula geometrically by interpreting holonomy as circulation in a map.
source_span.label: Chapter 27
source_span.title: Geometric Proof of the Metric Curvature Formula
topics: Circulation of vector fields around loops
Flat-plane holonomy as a dry run
Metric-induced vector fields in coordinate maps
Geometric derivation of the curvature formula
Why the metric alone determines curvature
